stories.title: Rockies 7, Cubs 3 — Rea Season-High 7 ER
stories.angle: Colin Rea allowed a season-high 7 ER in 4 2/3 IP; Rockies snapped four-game skid at Cubs' expense; Cubs fall to 34-33

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Colin Rea allowed a season-high 7 ER in 4 2/3 innings last night.

Rockies 7, Cubs 3. Coors Field is not being kind to this pitching staff — and the Cubs fall to 34-33.

Hunter Goodman's 2-run shot in the first set the tone. It only got worse from there.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Rotation Crisis — Taillon to IL, Three Starters Down
stories.angle: Taillon (hamstring, out through ASB) joins Horton (Tommy John) and Steele (UCL) on IL simultaneously; Boyd expected back this road trip

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
THREE Cubs starters are on the IL simultaneously: Horton (Tommy John), Steele (UCL), Taillon (hamstring, out through the All-Star break).

The rotation is in crisis. Matthew Boyd expected back on this road trip — and he can't get here fast enough.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

stories.title: Matt Shaw Activated, Alcántara Optioned
stories.angle: Shaw returns from 18-day IL stint (mid-back tightness); .242/.291/.400 on the season; Alcántara optioned to Iowa

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Matt Shaw is back on the active roster after 18 days with mid-back tightness.

He's slashing .242/.291/.400 with 3 HR and 12 RBI in 42 games. Not a superstar line, but depth matters when this roster is banged up.

Kevin Alcántara heads back to Iowa.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

stories.title: Ben Brown's All-Star Case Is Real
stories.angle: 1.74 ERA, 0.88 WHIP, 58 K in 57 IP — only Cubs starter under 4.70 ERA; zero HRs allowed in starts; NL All-Star candidate

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
Ben Brown: 1.74 ERA. 0.88 WHIP. 58 strikeouts in 57 innings.

The Cubs' rotation is falling apart around him, and Brown is quietly building one of the best pitching cases in the NL.

Zero home runs allowed in his starts. The All-Star argument is real.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

stories.title: Pete Crow-Armstrong — Sophomore Leap
stories.angle: .783 OPS, 11 HR, 14 SB through 277 PA — the brightest offensive spot on an injury-depleted roster

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
.783 OPS. 11 home runs. 14 stolen bases.

Pete Crow-Armstrong is becoming one of the most complete outfielders in baseball — and the Cubs desperately need him to keep being the story while the rotation burns.

Sophomore leap is fully underway.

#Cubs #GoCubs #ChicagoCubs

stories.title: NL Central Watch — Brewers Running Away
stories.angle: Cubs 34-33 vs Brewers 40-23; eight games back in division; wild card fight now more realistic than division title

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Cubs: 34-33. Brewers: 40-23. Eight games back in the division, and Milwaukee is not slowing down.

This is a wild card race now — and you can't win the wild card while dropping games to 25-win teams at Coors.

95 games left. Start playing like it.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Tonight — Imanaga vs Lorenzen at Coors Field
stories.angle: Cubs need bounce-back; Lorenzen 2-8, 8.01 ERA is one of NL's worst starters; Boyd potentially back Thursday

x_posts.label: Post 7A
x_posts.text:
Tonight at Coors: Shota Imanaga vs Michael Lorenzen, who is 2-8 with an 8.01 ERA.

The matchup favors Chicago on paper. The Cubs desperately need this win.

Boyd expected back Thursday. Time to salvage this road trip.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
